steps.title: Identify the known angles in the triangle
steps.content: From the diagram, the right angle is at $B$ (marked with a small square), so $\angle B = 90^{\circ}$. The angle at $A$ is labeled as $56^{\circ}$. We need to find $\angle C$.

steps.title: Apply the angle sum property of triangles
steps.content:
The sum of all angles in any triangle equals $180^{\circ}$:
$$\angle A + \angle B + \angle C = 180^{\circ}$$
$$56^{\circ} + 90^{\circ} + \angle C = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\angle C = 180^{\circ} - 146^{\circ} = 34^{\circ}$$
That confirms D is correct.
